name: Microsoft Teams
description: The collaboration layer many property teams use for internal coordination, quick escalations, meetings, file sharing, and live issue response.
whyUseIt: Commercial property work is cross-functional by nature. Teams keeps building ops, finance, leasing, client leads, and contractors aligned when fast communication matters and a long email chain would slow everything down.
aiCapabilities: AI can summarize meetings, turn discussions into actions, and help teams recover decisions buried inside long threads and calls.
workflowStage: Daily coordination, incident escalation, and client-team alignment
propertyManagerWorkflow: Property managers use Teams to coordinate contractor attendance, align internal stakeholders before owner meetings, and keep issue-response communication moving in real time.
managerExpects: Clear channels, concise updates, and documented decisions that can be found later instead of disappearing into chat noise.
aiWorkflowImpact: AI reduces recap time after meetings and helps convert conversation into accountable next actions more quickly.
demoDescription: Take a tenant-complaint thread, extract the decisions made, and convert them into a same-day action list with owners.

vocabulary.definition: A shared workspace for a team, building, client, or issue stream.
A running list of decisions, owners, and due dates created from meetings or issue threads.
A message stream used to coordinate urgent response and keep stakeholders aligned.

beginnerMistakes: Letting important approvals live only in chat with no recorded action owner
Posting long updates when a short summary and clear ask would work better
Using too many channels, which hides the signal
